programmes.show_name: And the Academy Award Goes To...
programmes.show_description: 1/3. 4 Extra Debut. An American crime classic. Paul Gambaccini on the story behind 1971 Best Picture winner, The French Connection. From 2016.
Patrick Wright investigates how bohemians and the military created together the first effective forms of camouflage. From 2002.
1/5. Miss Marple senses danger at a friend's manor house school for wayward boys. Agatha Christie's whodunnit stars June Whitfield.
1/5. The anonymous English hero is now in a German jail. But there's been an air raid. Sequel to Rogue Male read by Michael Jayston.
6/6. Fulton and Margaret prepare to rededicate their vows. Stars Robin Bailey and Doreen Mantle. From June 1980.
13/20. Butcher Jones is short of meat, so Walker arranges a special off-ration supply. Adapted from BBC TV with Clive Dunn. From 1975.
1/4. In attempting to write Sigmund Freud's biography, Boswell finds it's Freud asking all the questions. With Miles Jupp. From 2015.
1/5. While five-year old William fishes in the castle moat, his older brother's seizures are diagnosed. Memoir read by Dan Stevens.
1/5. Winter 1913 finds Frank Reid, the English owner of a Moscow printing company, abandoned by his wife. Narrated by Clare Higgins.
4/6. Nicholas Parsons chairs the devious word game. With Paul Merton, Julian Clary, Gyles Brandreth and Tony Hawks. From 2011.

programmes.show_name: The Six Mothers-In-Law Of Henry VIII
programmes.show_description: 2/6. 'Unreliable' historical account of Anne Boleyn's mother's impact on the court of Henry. Stars Diane Fletcher. From 2003.
Alan and Jean Warburton decide to spend their holiday in their bedroom. Poignant comedy drama starring Julie Hesmondhalgh.
At a newspaper office in Belfast, a sub-editor faces unrest in the city and her own life. Read by Aoibhéann McCann.
2/5. Spinster sleuth Jane Marple detects disquiet amongst an old school friend's family, and then there's a shooting. Stars June Whitfield.
2/5. Facing a perilous flight across occupied Europe, the aristocratic English hero must rely on his wits. Read by Michael Jayston.
5/8. East meets West in an epic chess game where politics, pride and chicken nuggets are at stake. Stars Dinsdale Landen. From April 1988.
2/6. It is 1942, and Neddie Seagoon must stop the Germans' secret weapon. Stars Spike Milligan and Peter Sellers. From December 1959.
4/6. Swift turns up at Edinburgh's story festival, where a mix up leads to a literary masterpiece. With Jessica Hardwick.  From 2016.
2/5. Eight-year-old William finds a secret door. Richard suffers dark mood swings but enjoys pipe smoking. Read by Dan Stevens.
2/5. Frank Reid is not being helped by his accountant, Selwyn. Selwyn writes poems. Stars Richard McCabe and David Bamber.
3/4. Alasdair Beckett-King hosts a panel of improv comedy all stars. Featuring some excited nuns, enemies who become lovers, and a fairy godmother.
4 Extra Debut. Alistair Cooke reflects on devastating effects of the war in Vietnam in his 1000th Letter from America. From 1968.

programmes.show_name: Jamie MacDonald: Life On The Blink
programmes.show_description: 2/4. Stand-up comedian Jamie MacDonald, who lives with his blindness, shares more hilarious anecdotes from his life. From 2023.
1/6. Joining the Sheffield singer-songwriter on-air are Barbara Dickson and Bill Bailey. Stars Graham Fellows. From February 2000.
